article_meta.title: IdeasWhat History Says Will Happen Next in IranMilitant groups have become more violent, specifically against civilians,

reasoning: Generally solid identification of bias in the author's prose with mostly appropriate labels, but one item includes quoted source language in the flagged span and several clear additional instances were missed.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: One flagged snippet embeds a quoted phrase (“a living martyr...”) within the span, violating the rule to avoid quoted language, even though the explanation focused on adjacent authorial phrasing.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Flagged items are largely genuine author-prose bias (e.g., ‘the record shows,’ ‘sprawling violent network,’ ‘promotes terrorism,’ colloquial ‘not blowing it up’). No clear overreach beyond the minor inclusion of quoted text.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed several defensible author-level bias instances such as ‘rudderless violence,’ ‘ruthless against civilians,’ ‘more radical,’ ‘maximum carnage,’ ‘so-called leadership decapitation,’ ‘paroxysm of violence,’ and ‘paying the price.’
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are mostly appropriate or defensible: Opinion-as-fact for declarative generalizations; Subjective Qualifying Adjectives for ‘sprawling’; Spin for interpretive/colloquial causation. One could argue Sensationalism for ‘not blowing it up,’ but Spin is still plausible.
